Read /workspace/POLARIS/docs/agentic_outline_redesign.md (read-only) for the binding T1/T2 spec.
SUCCESS IS MEASURED BY: (a) A computed number (e.g. a delta/ratio) can be COMPUTED by the agent AND RENDER in the outline via the verified [#calc:] lane (quantified_analysis.py:617), never via [CITE:ev_xxx] — proven end-to-end on one real case; (b) the T1 toolkit tools land and pass real unit tests; (c) the T2 battery runs and S0/S1 failures trend to zero. Faithfulness invariant is the hard gate: NO derived/unverified number may ever reach the [CITE:ev_xxx] render path (that would be a P0 breach).
The bar is not "bug fixed" — it is the benchmark number at the top, VERIFIED. Keep going until the
measurement says top (or you hit a hard external blocker you must report honestly).

FENCE (military discipline — no exceptions):
- Work ONLY inside /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent (branch bot/outline-agent-box). NEVER touch another worktree.
- NEVER edit anything under /home/polaris/polaris_project (operator's monitoring dir — deck.sh, wheel*.js, wheels.py).
  If you catch yourself editing a monitoring script or a workflow .js, you have DRIFTED — stop.
- Report REAL output only. Never invent, never claim a pass you did not observe, never fake a benchmark number.
- Commit every landed step in /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent; append one dated line to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md.


YOU ARE OPUS — the hands AND the eyes. Do the hard work yourself: read the exact files named, search
where needed, build, fix, run, MEASURE. Do not wait for anyone to read the code for you.

CURRENT STATE (accurate as of wheel start — verify, don't assume):
worktree /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent @ HEAD 98726ca (branch bot/outline-agent-box). Inherited work is SOLID and PASSES: 19 tests green in ~2.8s. execute_python is un-bricked. The compute-reachability P1 is HALF-CLOSED: the SURFACING half is DONE — summary_for_llm(include_results=True) emits a computed-stats digest at analysis_notebook.py:227-276. The RENDER half is STILL OPEN at outline_agent.py:1609 — a computed number cannot yet render through the verified [#calc:] lane. NOT BUILT YET: src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_toolkit.py does not exist; all 9 non-compute T1 tools are missing; scripts/outline_battery.py does not exist; the fold-in seam (_offset_renumber outline_agent.py:610 + _stamp_and_delete :792) is NOT yet factored as a reusable unit.

YOUR ACTIONS THIS ROUND (precise; do these, in order, then MEASURE):
Fable's gate verdict was CONTINUE. Reason: Diff verified correct by real runs this round (111/111 tests, battery 11/11 S0..S4=0, both re-executed by me, plus real-corpus payload inspection settling the layering question). Faithfulness intact: DOI fold is planner-prompt-only, calc_claims change is homing-only and still gated by the registry-verified [#calc:] lane; [CITE:ev_xxx] path untouched. Not at top because the mission's full-corpus live benchmark number is unproducible without OPENROUTER_API_KEY — a measurement Opus could not take is not a sign-off.
DO THIS NEXT (precise): RULING: keep the len(stamped)>=2 refusal at outline_digest.py:297 — it is the correct conservative choice, and the worry-case premise is empirically false: the real cp3 payload (/workspace/POLARIS/outputs/s3_gear/cp3_basket_snapshot.json) contains NO singleton groups (all 55 groups have >=2 members), so a one-row-per-URL same-DOI pair arrives UNCLAIMED and the fold already merges it (the measured 3->1 case); the refusal only bites when two MULTI-member url: groups share a DOI, which has zero incidence in the real corpus (5/654 rows have usable DOIs; 0 DOIs span >=2 cp3 groups), and a member-level override would SPLIT a cp3 group (non-DOI siblings left on the old key) unless key-level union-find remapping were built — unjustified for zero incidence and it would make the digest override cp3's deliberate url-first STEP-4 identity. Next action (one small commit): lock the ruling with (1) a test in tests/polaris_graph/test_outline_digest.py encoding the two-multi-member-url-groups-shared-DOI case and asserting NO merge (stamped members keep their url: keys, unclaimed members fold to doi:), and (2) a cheap observability tripwire — count DOI groups hitting the len(stamped)>=2 branch and surface it (e.g. in corroboration_profile or a digest stat + a per-basket log line in scripts/outline_agentic_sweep.py) so if the zero-incidence assumption breaks on the 346-basket corpus it is visible, not silent. Then the wheel is code-complete: keep the OPENROUTER_API_KEY escalation standing in WHEEL_PROGRESS.md as the sole gate and run the staged sweep the moment the key lands — never proxy the live number.
Gap to top: Offline metrics are at ceiling and re-verified this round (111/111 outline+digest tests; T2 battery 11/11 with S0..S4=0; [#calc:] lane proven end-to-end on the live-shaped composer path in prior rounds). This is a hard external blocker, not a code gap — the staged sweep (PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1 python scripts/outline_agentic_sweep.py) fires the instant credentials land.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).

- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

112	    singleton_lines: list[str]              # one line per row in no multi-member cluster
113	    ev_id_to_basket: dict[str, str]         # member ev_id -> basket id (full map; never elided)
114	    total_chars: int                        # rendered menu size (headroom accounting)

117	    # pathological pool that would ship a silently oversized prompt. Disclosed (never silent): the
118	    # builder also logs a WARNING, and the caller must surface/act on this flag (fail loud).
119	    oversized: bool = False

123	    # ``same_work_groups`` was not supplied. ``covered_ev_ids()`` unions these so every alias is
124	    # still accounted for by the 100%-of-pool invariant (§-1.3 consolidate — folded, never dropped).
125	    singleton_alias_ev_ids: dict[str, list[str]] = field(default_factory=dict)

127	    # deduped through the same-work alias map). Equals the row count when no members share a work
128	    # (so it is byte-identical to ``len(members)`` when ``same_work_groups`` is absent). This is the

134	        return "\n".join(self.basket_lines + self.singleton_lines)

136	    def covered_ev_ids(self) -> set[str]:
137	        """Every ev_id the menu accounts for (basket member OR singleton OR a folded alias)."""
138	        covered = set(self.ev_id_to_basket)
139	        for line in self.singleton_lines:
140	            # singleton lines start with the canonical ev_id token (see _singleton_line)
141	            covered.add(line.split(" ", 1)[0])

143	        # union them explicitly (the line only prints the canonical head token).
144	        for canonical, aliases in self.singleton_alias_ev_ids.items():

163	# normalized to a work key. "(PDF) GPTs are GPTs" must key IDENTICALLY to "GPTs are GPTs" — the
164	# leading "pdf" is alphanumeric, so it survives the alnum-normalize and would otherwise SPLIT the
165	# same work into two. Exact known tokens only (content-integrity, not a lexical quality guess).
166	_TITLE_CHROME_PREFIXES = ("[pdf]", "(pdf)", "url source:", "pdf source:")
167

169	def _strip_title_chrome(title: str) -> str:
170	    """Peel leading chrome tokens (``[pdf]`` / ``(pdf)`` / ``url source:``) and trailing ellipsis
171	    (``...`` / unicode ``…``) off a title so a chrome-prefixed or truncated title normalizes to the
172	    SAME key as its clean form (item 3a). Loops so stacked prefixes ("[PDF] URL Source:") all peel.
173	    Never drops the row — only cleans the KEY input (§-1.3: a fold keeps every row, it only reports
174	    them as one work)."""
175	    t = (title or "").strip()
176	    changed = True
177	    while changed:
178	        changed = False
179	        low = t.lower()
180	        for pref in _TITLE_CHROME_PREFIXES:
181	            if low.startswith(pref):
182	                t = t[len(pref):].strip()
183	                changed = True
184	                break
185	    while t.endswith("...") or t.endswith("…"):
186	        t = (t[:-3] if t.endswith("...") else t[:-1]).rstrip()
187	    return t

190	# Item 5 (same-work DOI fold): a byte-for-byte mirror of ``finding_dedup._normalize_doi`` /
191	# ``weighted_enrichment._normalize_doi``. This module deliberately never imports finding_dedup
192	# (LAW V one-responsibility, zero generator-package deps), so the shared DOI contract is copied
193	# here verbatim. Keep in lockstep with finding_dedup:_DOI_PREFIX_RE / _normalize_doi.
194	_DOI_PREFIX_RE = re.compile(r"^(?:doi:|https?://(?:dx\.)?doi\.org/)", re.IGNORECASE)
195

197	def _normalize_doi(doi: Any) -> str:
198	    """Canonical DOI for same-work grouping (SHARED contract with finding_dedup._normalize_doi).
199	
200	    Lowercase → strip a leading ``doi:`` / ``https://doi.org/`` / ``http://dx.doi.org/`` prefix →
201	    trim → ``rstrip("/")``. A usable DOI starts with the ``10.`` registrant prefix; anything else
202	    is noise. Returns ``""`` for a missing / blank / non-``10.`` DOI (it never groups two works)."""
203	    text = str(doi or "").strip().lower()
204	    if not text:
205	        return ""
206	    text = _DOI_PREFIX_RE.sub("", text).strip().rstrip("/")
207	    return text if text.startswith("10.") else ""

210	def _normalized_title_key(title: str) -> str | None:
211	    """Item 2 (same-work TITLE fallback): a work_key derived from a source TITLE, for folding
212	    TITLE-IDENTICAL works the cp3 URL/DOI ``same_work_groups`` missed (the same paper posted at two
213	    URLs — an arXiv PDF + a GovAI mirror of "GPTs are GPTs", or Noy_Zhang_1.pdf + "Experimental
214	    Evidence on the Productivity Effects..."). Lowercase, keep only alphanumerics. Returns
215	    ``title:<norm>`` ONLY when the normalized title is >= ``_TITLE_FALLBACK_MIN_CHARS`` — a
216	    short/generic title (two DIFFERENT OECD reg-policy docs) returns ``None`` and is NEVER folded,
217	    so the fallback cannot false-merge distinct works. Never a drop (§-1.3): a fold keeps ALL rows,
218	    it only reports them as ONE work for corroboration.
219	
220	    Item 3a: leading fetch/format chrome ("(PDF)", "URL Source:") and trailing ellipsis are peeled
221	    FIRST (``_strip_title_chrome``), so a chrome-prefixed or truncated title keys IDENTICALLY to its
222	    clean form instead of splitting the same underlying work into two."""
223	    norm = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]", "", _strip_title_chrome(title).lower())
224	    if len(norm) < _TITLE_FALLBACK_MIN_CHARS:
225	        return None
226	    return f"title:{norm}"

523	def build_outline_digest(
524	    evidence: Sequence[Mapping[str, Any]],
525	    clusters: Sequence[Any],
526	    *,
527	    max_chars: int | None = None,
528	    sanitizer: Callable[[str], tuple[str, int]] | None = None,
529	    same_work_groups: Sequence[Mapping[str, Any]] | None = None,
530	    prioritize_tier1: bool = False,
531	) -> OutlineDigestMenu:
532	    """Build the basket-digest menu from the evidence pool + finding clusters.
533	
534	    ``clusters`` is duck-typed over ``FindingCluster`` (``representative_index``,
535	    ``member_indices``, ``corroboration_count``, ``member_hosts``) so this module never
536	    imports finding_dedup. A cluster with >= 2 members becomes a BASKET line; every other
537	    pool row becomes a SINGLETON line — so the menu accounts for 100% of the pool
538	    (invariant, asserted below; §-1.3 CONSOLIDATE-keep-all, zero rows dropped).
539	
540	    ``same_work_groups`` (PUSH A, OPTIONAL — the exact cp3 ``payload.same_work_groups`` shape:
541	    ``{member_evidence_ids, canonical_index, same_work_id}``). When supplied, an alias map
542	    ev_id->work_key is built; basket lines then report corroboration at WORK level (``xK works
543	    (N rows)``) with every member id still listed, and same-work SINGLETON copies collapse into
544	    ONE line (canonical ev_id + alias ids). ``None`` (default) => byte-identical to the
545	    pre-PUSH-A menu.
546	
547	    Headroom guard (Design 5 ORCH-1): if the rendered menu exceeds ``max_chars`` the
548	    singleton statements terse away FIRST (row kept), then basket member lists elide to
549	    counts (the ev_id -> basket map is ALWAYS preserved) — content degrades gracefully; no
550	    row ever leaves the menu.
551	    """
552	    if sanitizer is None:
553	        sanitizer = _default_sanitizer()
554	    if max_chars is None:
555	        max_chars = _env_int("PG_OUTLINE_DIGEST_MAX_CHARS", PG_OUTLINE_DIGEST_MAX_CHARS_DEFAULT)
556	
557	    # PUSH A: alias map ev_id -> work_key. ``work_aware`` gates the honest work-level render so
558	    # ``same_work_groups=None`` stays byte-identical (an explicit ``[]`` opts into the new render
559	    # with an empty alias map => work count == row count, zero singleton folds).
560	    work_aware = same_work_groups is not None
561	    # Item 2: feed the pool so ``_build_alias_map`` also folds TITLE-identical works the cp3 groups
562	    # missed — but ONLY on the work-aware path, so ``same_work_groups=None`` stays byte-identical.
563	    alias_of = _build_alias_map(same_work_groups, evidence if work_aware else None)
564	
565	    def _work_key(ev_id: str) -> str:
566	        return alias_of.get(ev_id, ev_id)
567	
568	    def _clean(text: str) -> str:
569	        return sanitizer(text or "")[0]
570	
571	    # ── 1. multi-member baskets, deterministically ordered (heaviest corroboration first,
572	    #        ties by representative ev_id then member set) so line order is worker-independent.
573	    multi = [c for c in clusters if len(getattr(c, "member_indices", []) or []) >= 2]
574	
575	    def _sort_key(c: Any) -> tuple[Any, ...]:
576	        _member_ev = [e for e in (_ev_id_at(evidence, i) for i in getattr(c, "member_indices")) if e]
577	        rep = _ev_id_at(evidence, int(getattr(c, "representative_index")))
578	        members = tuple(sorted(_member_ev))
579	        if work_aware:
580	            # Item 5: order baskets by WORK-level corroboration (distinct works), NOT raw ROW count.
581	            # B00 must be the basket backed by the MOST distinct works — 4 duplicate rows of ONE work
582	            # (work_corroboration=1) must sink BELOW a genuine 2-work basket. 44 of 69 baskets this
583	            # run were x1-work clusters leading the menu by row count. Tie-break by row count, then
584	            # rep + member set for worker-independent determinism.
585	            #
586	            # Item 6c composed with item 5: a basket whose representative row is a fetch interstitial
587	            # ("Just a moment..." Cloudflare) is a failed fetch, NOT corroboration — its members are N
588	            # distinct FAILED urls, which would otherwise float it to the TOP of the menu by work
589	            # count (the exact opposite of item 6c's "stop selling chrome to the planner"). Sink such
590	            # baskets BELOW every real basket (leading sort dim), keeping work-sort WITHIN the real and
591	            # within the chrome groups. Still KEPT + tagged (disclosure, §-1.3.1(a)) — only its RANK
592	            # drops. Non-work-aware path is byte-identical (this whole branch is work-aware only).
593	            _rep_title = str(evidence[int(getattr(c, "representative_index"))].get("title", "") or "")
594	            _chrome = 1 if _is_chrome_interstitial(_rep_title) else 0
595	            _wc = len({_work_key(e) for e in _member_ev})
596	            return (_chrome, -_wc, -len(_member_ev), rep, members)
597	        return (-int(getattr(c, "corroboration_count", 0)), rep, members)
